creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Nikos from comment #6)
> added from this site http://www.aossama.com/installing-asterisk-on-freebsd/
> my confugiration
> 
> try to recompile port and started gdb but says (gdb) bt
> No stack.
> 
> open firefox http://localhost:8088/aserisk and says not found after this
> closed service

I tested following these steps using the binary package from the official FreeBSD mirrors and the web interface works, I cannot access the static pages, and there could be a bug there in the port I'm going to investigate, but other standard asterisk urls like http://localhost:8088/asterisk/httpstatus are working fine and asterisk is not crashing.

I suspect you have a problem with your locally build package.

Could you attach the output of "pkg info"(which lists all packages installed on a machine) and "pkg info -d asterisk13" (which will list the packages asterisk13 is depending upon).

My main suspect is you have a mix of base OpenSSL and some other ports provided ssl library installed and asterisk is dynamically linking to both, causing the crash.

To further check this you could also attach the output of:

ldd /usr/local/sbin/asterisk
ldd /usr/local/lib/asterisk/modules/res_curl.so
ldd /usr/local/lib/libcurl.so


these will show the libraries used by those binaries, in this way I should be able to see if there is a mismatch.
